description_long: This structure is a traditional pagoda with two prominent stories, each defined by wide, overhanging roofs with gently upturned eaves. The lower level features a broad balcony with railings and a series of sliding panel doors, reminiscent of Japanese shoji screens, set atop a concrete base. The upper story is smaller, with a wraparound balcony and a solid parapet, topped by a steeply pitched, four-sided roof. The roof surfaces are finished in a reddish-brown material, likely tile or wood, and the overall color palette is earthy and muted. The building's symmetry and clean lines evoke a sense of calm and order, while the upward-curving eaves add a touch of elegance. There is no visible ornamental finial at the apex of the roof, keeping the design simple and understated.
description: A two-story traditional pagoda with wide, upturned eaves, wraparound balconies, and sliding panel doors, finished in earthy tones. The structure features a steep, reddish-brown roof and a solid, symmetrical design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with sliding panel doors, a broad balcony, and the layered roof structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wide, upturned eaves and the full length of the balcony railings on both stories.
description_view_2: Rear view with a solid parapet on the upper story and a less detailed lower level, emphasizing the building's symmetry.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the roof's curvature and balcony railings.
